California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
April 11, 19 91
Bradley w. Hertz
REED & DAVIDSON
550 South Flower Street, 7th Floor
Los Angeles, CA 9OO7l
Re: Your Request for Informaf Assistance
our FiLe No. I-9 0-6 3 3
Dear Mr. Heri-z3
You have requested advice regardj-ng the conf l"ict of j.nterest
provisions of the Politicat Reform Act (the "Act").1 
Since your
]etter does not involve a specific governmental decision, we are
treating your request as one for inforrnal assistance. z
OUESTION
Is a purchaser of advertising space fron a nelrspaper a source
of income to the or^rner of the nel^rspaper, and, if so, woul-d the
ner^/spaper be considered a retail business subject to the exception
for income from retail sales found in section 87103.5?
CONCLUS ION
A purchaser of advertising space from a newspaper is a source
of incorne to the owner of the newspaper. However, the exception
found j-n Section 87103.5 for retail sales applies to the saLe of
nehrspaper advertising space. Therefore, the ol^rner of the
1 cor"..,."nt code Sections 81ooo-91015. A11 statutory references
are to the Governnent code unl-ess otherwise indi-cated. Comrnission
regulations appear at 2 cafifornia code of Regu1ations. Section
18000, et seq. Afl references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California code of Regulations.
2 Informal- assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(covernment code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Section l-832e (c) (3 ) . )
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4-O8O7 o (916)322-5660


Our File No. I-9 0-6 3 3
Page 2
newspaper would not have to disqualify hirnself from decisions
affecting an advertiser if the retail- customers of the newspaper
constitute a significant segment of the public generally, and the
amount of income received by the ne$rspaper from the advertiser is
not distingruishable from the amount of income received from its
other retail customers.
FACTS
Your client is a city councilmember $rho owns and control-s a
r^/eekly newspaper. A corporation which advertises in the newspaper
may come before the city council on a matter which will have a
materiaf financiaL effect on that corporation. The advertising
purchased in the twelve months preceding the council decision
amounts to more than $250.
You are questioning whether the corporation is a source of
income to the ne$/spaper or./ner and, if so, whether the exception
found j.n section 87103.5 for income from retail sales would aPPfY,
so that the councilmember could participate in a decision
materially affecting the corporation.
ANALYS I S
As a city councilrnember, your client is a public official 
of
a }ocal government agency (see Sections 82048 and 82041) and is,
therefore, subject to the conflict of interest provisions of the
Act ,
Section 871-00 provides that "In]o public official 
at any
Ievel of state or Ioca1 governrnent shall rnake, participate in
makj.ng or in any way use his official 
position to influence a
governmentaf decisi-on in which he knows or has reason to know he
has a financial interest." 
Section 87103 defines t'financial-
interest", in pertinent part, as follows:
An official 
has a financiaf interest in a
decision within the rneaning of Section 87100 if it
is reasonably foreseeable that the decisj-on will
have a material financial effect, distinguishable
frorn its effect on the public generally, on:
(c) Any source of income, other than loans by
a commercial lending institution in the regular
course of business, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value received by or
promised to the public officiat within tweLve
months prior to the tirne when the decision is nade;


our File No. f-90-633
Page 3
You have not indicated that there are olrners of the neirrspaper
other than the councilmember you represent. we assume that the
council-member o$rns 10 percent or more of the newspaper.
Therefore, any source of income to the newspaper in the twelve
months preceding a governmental decision woul-d be considered a
source of income to the counci hnernber, if his prorata share of the
payments was $2so or more. (Sections 82030 and 87103 (c) . )
However, as you have correctly noted in your letter, there
is, in Section 87103.5, an exception to Section 87103(c) for
income f rorn retail sales.
Section 87103.5 sets forth the following:
Notwithstanding subdivision (c) of section
a7lo3, a retail customer of a business entity
engaged in retail sales of goods or services to the
public generally is not a source of income to an
official 
who owns a 1o-percent or greater interest
in the entity if the retail customers of the
business entity constitute a significant segment of
the public generaLLy, and the amount of incorne
received by the business entity from the customer
.is not dist j-nguishabl-e fron the amount of income
received from its other retail customers.
A newspaper, which sel-ls space to advertj,sers and provides
distribution to the public of the advertisements placed by
advertising customers, is considered a retaiL business for
purposes of Section 871,03 . 5 .
Regulation 18703.5, copy enclosed, clarifies Section 87103.5
as follows:
(a) For purposes of Government Code Section
87103.5, the retail- customers of a business entity
constitute a significant segment of the public
generally if either of the following is true:
(1) The retail customers of the busi.ness
entity during the preceding 12 months are
sufficient in nurnber to equaf 10 percent or
nore of the population or households of the
jurisdiction; 
or
(2) The retail customers of the business
entity during the preceding 12 months number
at least ten thousand.
For purposes of this subdivision, a custoner
of a retail busj-ness entity is each separate and
distinct purchaser of goods or services, whether an


our File No. I-9 o-63 3
Page 4
individual, household, business or other entity.
If records are not maintained by customer name, a
good faith estj-mate shall be made to determine !'rhat
percentage of sal-es transactj,ons represent nultiple
transactions by repeat custorners. The total number
of sales transactions shall then be reduced by the
estimated percentage of repeat customers to yield
the number of customers for purposes of applying
this subdivis ion.
(b) For purposes of Government Code Section
87103.5, the amount of income received by a
business entity from a retail customer is not
distinguishable from the amount of income received
from its other retail customers if the amount spent
by the customer in question during the preceding 12
months is less than one-tenth of 1 percent of the
gross sales revenues of the retail business entity
for the preceding fiscal year.
(c) For purposes of Government Code Section
87100, an official 
who owns 10 percent or more of a
retai.l business entity \"rhich neets the criteria in
either subdj-vision (a) (l) or (a) (z) , does not I'have
reason to knowfi that a decision wi]-I affect a
source of income to the retail business entity when
either of the following applies:
(1) If all of the following are true:
(A) The customer does not have a
charge account or open book account with
the retail business;
(B) The retail business does not
rnaintain records for noncharge customer
transactions by customer name or other
method for tracking transactions which
would provide the customer name; and
(c) The fact that the person is a
customer is not personally known to the
of f icial,' or
(2) If all of the following are true:
(A) The accounts and books of the
retail business entity are maintained by
soneone other than the official 
or a
member of the official's 
immediate
fanily; and


Our File No. I -9 O-63 3
Page 5
(B) The fact that the person is a
customer is not personal,ly known to the
official.
(d) For purposes of subdivision (c), a credit
card transaction utilizing 
a credit card not issued
by the retail business entity is considered a
trnoncharge customer transaction.tl
(e) Subdivision (c) shalI not be utilized in
determining e/hether an official 
trknowsfl of a
financial j-nterest in a decision within the rneaning
of Government Code Secti-on 87100. When such
knowledge exists, or the fact that a person is a
source of incorne i.s brought to the attention of the
official 
prior to the governmental decision, the
provisions of subdivision (c) sha11 have no effect
on the official-'s duty to disqualify.
Thus, as per Regulation 18703.5(b), if the corporation that
advertises in the subject newspaper provides less than one-tenth
of one percent of the gross advertising sales revenue of the
newspaper, the income from the corporation would be considered to
be indistinguishable from income provided by other retail
customers, and the city councilmember would not have to disqualify
himself or herself from participating in decisions which would
have a reasonably foreseeable rnaterial financial- effect on the
corporation. Bear in rnind, however, that if the cj-rculation of
the ner^/spaper anounts to fewer persons than are required by
Regulation 18703.5(a) (1) and (2), the statutory exceptj-on for
owners of retail, businesses would not aPPIY, and the corporation
would be considered a source of incorne to the off ici-al- under
87103 (c) .
If the criteria for Regulation 18703.5 are not met, then it
must be determined $rhether a governmental decision would have a
reasonably foreseeabl-e, material f j-nancial effect upon the source
of income to the Public official.
whether the financial consequences of a decision are reason-
ably foreseeable at the tirne a governmental decision is rnade
deplnas on the facts of each particufar case. An effect is
coi-rsidered reasonably foreseeibfe if there is a substantial
Iikelihood that it wi]1, occur. certainty is not required'
ior"r.., 
if an effect is only a mere possibility, 
it is not
."a=on.bly foreseeabLe- (In re Thorner (1975) 1 FPPC ops' 198,
copy enclosed. )


our File No. I-9 0-63 3
Page 6
In addition, the foreseeable effect on the counciLmember's
source of income must also be material to require
disqua l ification. 
The test for material f j.nancial effect differs
depending on the specific circumstances of each decision. For
exanple, where a source of j-ncome is directly before the city
council, Regulation la702- 1(a) (copy enclosed) provides that the
any financial effect of the decision on a source of income is
de6ned material and disqualification j.s required.3 (combs Advice
Letter, No. A-89-177, copy enclosed.)
Where the source of income j-s not directly before the city
council, but may be indj.rectly affected, ReguLations 18702.2 and
18702.6 (copies enclosed) apply. Regulation 18702- 5 applies to
situations where a decision affects an individual- who is a source
of income of $250 or more. Regulation 78702.2 provides different
standards of materiality which apply to sources of income that are
business entities depending upon the size of the enti-ty.
Eor example, negulation 18702.2(q) provides materiality
standards for smafler business entities as folfows:
(1) The decision will resul-t in an increase
or decrease in the gross revenues for a fiscal year
of $10,000 or more; or
(2) The decision wiII resuLt in the business
entity incurring or avoiding additional- expenses or
reducing or elj-rninating existing expenses for a
fiscal year in the amount of 52,500 or nore; or
(3) The decision will- result in the increase
or decrease in the value of assets or liabilities
of $10,000 or more.
Finatly, where a source of j-ncome is an individual and not
directly before the city council, Regulation 78702.6 provides:
The effect of a deci-sion i-s material as to an
individual- who is a source of income or gifts to an
official 
if any of the following applies:
(a) The decision will affect the
individual's income, investments, or other
tanqrible or intangj-bIe assets or ]iabj-lities
3 A =or.". of incorne is directly before the city council vrhen the
source initiates the proceeding by fil,ing an appJ.ication, claim,
appeat, or sj.rnil,ar request, or is a named party in, or the subject
of, the proceeding. A person or business entity is the subject of
a proceeding if a decision involves the issuance, renewal,
approval-, denial or revocation of any license, permit, or other
entitlement to, or contract with, the subject person or business
entity. (Regulation 18702.1(b) . )


our File No.
Page 7
I-9 0-5 3 3
(other than reaf property) by $1,000 or more;
or
(b) The decision will affect the
individual's real property interest in a
manner that is considered material- under
Section La702.3 or Section L8702.4.1
rn conclusion, the city councilmember nay not participate in
any decision in which a source of income of 9250 or more in the
preceding 12 months is directly involved and it is foreseeable
that there will be a financial effect on the source. In addition,
the councj.lmember may not participate in any decision that will
foreseeably and materj.ally affect a source of incorne indirectly.
The materiality of the effect would be determined by examining the
extent of the foreseeable financial effect on the source of
income.
The PubIic GeneraIIv Exception
Even if the reasonably foreseeable financial effect of a
decision is rnaterial, disqual ification is required only if the
effect of the decision is distinguishabl-e fron the effect upon the
public general-Iy.
For the 'tpublic generalLy't exception to apply, a decision
must affect the official's 
interests in substantially the same
manner as it would affect a significant seqment of the public.
(Regulation ]-8703, copy enclosed. ) The ttpublicrr consists of the
entire jurisdiction of the agency in question. (In re owen (1976)
2 FPPC ops.77, copy enclosed. ) this is so because aLl- the
residents of the jurisdiction are constituents of the officiaf.
I hope the above answers your question. If you require
further inforrnation please do not hesitate to contact ne at (916)
322-590]_.
S incere Iy ,
SH:SB:ken
Enc losures
4 Regulation 18702.3 and 18702.4 have been enclosed for your
inforrnation.
RIN
ra1 counsel
aI Division

QUESTION: Is a purchaser of advertising space fron a nelrspaper a source
of income to the or^rner of the nel^rspaper, and, if so, woul-d the
ner^/spaper be considered a retail business subject to the exception
for income from retail sales found in section 87103.5?

CONCLUSION: A purchaser of advertising space from a newspaper is a source
of incorne to the owner of the newspaper. However, the exception
found j-n Section 87103.5 for retail sales applies to the saLe of
nehrspaper advertising space. Therefore, the ol^rner of the

are to the Governnent code unl-ess otherwise indi-cated. Comrnission
regulations appear at 2 cafifornia code of Regu1ations. Section
18000, et seq. Afl references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California code of Regulations.
